comprehensive_summary:
Dante Alighieri is a medieval Italian poet best known for The Divine Comedy, an epic poem in three parts (Inferno, Purgatorio, Paradiso) written in terza rima, a verse form he invented consisting of stanzas with 11-syllable lines where the first and third lines rhyme and the second line rhymes with the first and last lines of the next stanza.

The Divine Comedy opens 'Midway through life's journey' with the narrator finding himself in 'a forest dark,' where he encounters a lion, a leopard, and a she-wolf. The inscription 'Abandon all hope, ye who enter here' marks the entrance to Hell. Virgil guides Dante through the Inferno and Purgatorio, while Beatrice guides him through Paradiso. Gustave Dore created famous illustrations of the poem, including one of Phlegyas rowing Dante and Virgil across the river Styx in Canto VIII. The poem was illustrated by Domenico di Michelino in Florence Cathedral. Henry Wadsworth Longfellow made an important English translation.

In the Inferno, Canto V features Francesca da Rimini, who shares an adulterous kiss while reading about 'Lancelot, how Love did him enthrall.' Dante asks her to relate how 'Love conceded that you should know your dubious desires.' The 'Canto of Ulysses' (Canto XXVI) features Ulysses declaring 'you were made men / To follow after knowledge and excellence' -- Primo Levi famously describes reciting this canto from memory in Auschwitz. Ugolino bites down on his own fingers in the lower circles. The first circle contains Limbo, populated with virtuous pagans. The city of Dis features a burning background. Lars von Trier based The House that Jack Built on the poem, and Pasolini's Salo takes its section titles from it.

In the Paradiso, Dante meets his great-great-grandfather Cacciaguida on Mars (the fifth sphere), who identifies souls forming the shape of a Greek cross. Beatrice explains the spots of the Moon to Dante in the second canto. The work's cosmology adapted the concept of the Empyrean.

In the Purgatorio, the troubadour Sordello appears. Thomas Merton's The Seven Storey Mountain is titled in reference to Dante's Purgatory.

La Vita Nuova is Dante's early collection of mixed prose and poetry (a prosimetrum) written in the 'sweet new style' (dolce stil novo). It describes his first sight of Beatrice Portinari. The character Love confronts the narrator while clothed in a fiery cloud, saying 'Ego dominus tuus' ('I am your master'). In a vision, Love feeds Dante's heart to Beatrice. Dante writes 'Love hath so long possessed me for his own.' The work begins by noting the 'incipit' in 'that part of the book of my memory.' Dante Gabriel Rossetti made an 1861 translation and painted Elizabeth Siddal as Beatrice.

The Convivio ('The Banquet') is an unfinished work featuring two sections themed on a Boethius-inspired praise for Lady Philosophy, referring to the traditional account that Eros is the son of Venus.

Dante was a close friend of Guido Cavalcanti, a fellow dolce stil novo poet. T.S. Eliot was obsessed with Dante: 'The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock' opens with a six-line quote from the Inferno; 'The Waste Land' calls Ezra Pound 'the better craftsman' referencing Dante; 'Little Gidding' employs terza rima; and Eliot wrote essays titled 'What Dante Means to Me' and simply 'Dante.' Samuel Beckett wrote an essay titled 'Dante... Bruno. Vico.. Joyce' and the story 'Dante and the Lobster.' Auguste Rodin's Gates of Hell depicts scenes from the Inferno including The Kiss, Ugolino and His Children, and The Thinker (originally based on Dante). Delacroix's Barque of Dante shows Dante and Virgil crossing the Styx. Bouguereau submitted a painting of a nude man biting another man's throat (from the Inferno) for the Prix de Rome. Liszt composed both a Dante Sonata and a Dante Symphony. Graham Harman wrote about the 'broken hammer' of Dante. Mary Jo Bang made a translation alluding to Elton John. Christina Rossetti's sister Maria Francesca wrote a study titled The Shadow of Dante.

works.name: The Divine Comedy
works.description: An epic poem in three parts (Inferno, Purgatorio, Paradiso) written in terza rima. Virgil guides Dante through Hell and Purgatory; Beatrice guides him through Paradise. Opens 'Midway through life's journey.'

clues.clue: Written in terza rima, a verse form Dante invented
quotes: this poet credited with inventing terza rima to write The Divine Comedy
Eliot's poem 'Little Gidding' which employs a terza rima rhyme scheme

clues.clue: Opens 'Midway through life's journey' with narrator in 'a forest dark'; encounters a lion, leopard, and she-wolf
quotes: that poem by this author opens 'Midway through life's journey,' after which the narrator encounters a lion, a leopard, and a she-wolf

clues.clue: 'Abandon all hope, ye who enter here' inscribed at Hell's entrance
quotes: A quote from this work is inscribed on the bucket of feces that Swoff burns in Jarhead
a phrase by this author is pointed to by three figures hanging their heads together

clues.clue: Virgil guides through Inferno/Purgatorio; Beatrice guides through Paradiso
quotes: in a poem's third section, guides this poet through paradise
Beatrice explains the spots of the Moon to Dante in the second canto

clues.clue: Inferno, Canto V: Francesca da Rimini shares adulterous kiss while reading about Lancelot
quotes: This woman shares an adulterous kiss while reading about 'Lancelot, how Love did him enthrall'

clues.clue: Inferno, Canto XXVI: 'Canto of Ulysses' -- 'you were made men / To follow after knowledge and excellence'
quotes: reciting this character's line 'you were made men / To follow after knowledge and excellence' from memory

clues.clue: First circle of Hell is Limbo, populated by virtuous pagans; unusual inclusion of apathetic souls
quotes: Dante's unusual choice to populate this place in Hell's first circle with virtuous pagans and apathetic souls

clues.clue: Paradiso: Dante meets great-great-grandfather Cacciaguida on Mars (fifth sphere); souls form a Greek cross
quotes: Dante meets his great-great-grandfather Cacciaguida, who identifies the names of the various souls forming the shape of a Greek cross

clues.clue: Gustave Dore's illustrations; Phlegyas rowing across the Styx in Canto VIII
quotes: its illustrations by Gustave Dore
Gustave Dore showed a man in a toga and laurel crown at this location being led by Phlegyas

clues.clue: Longfellow translated the poem
quotes: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow translated a poem by this author

clues.clue: Sordello the troubadour appears in Purgatorio (connects to Robert Browning's poem 'Sordello')
quotes: Robert Browning titled his poem 'Sordello' for a troubadour who appears in Purgatorio

clues.clue: Cosmology adapted the concept of the Empyrean
quotes: this medieval author, whose cosmology adapted the concept of the Empyrean

clues.clue: Pasolini's Salo takes section titles from it (e.g. 'Circle of Blood,' 'Antinferno')
quotes: inspired the titles of Salo's four parts, like 'Circle of Blood' and 'Antinferno'

clues.clue: Lars von Trier based The House that Jack Built on it
quotes: Lars von Trier based on this work

clues.clue: Rodin's Gates of Hell: includes The Kiss, Ugolino and His Children, The Thinker (originally based on Dante), Three Shades
quotes: A sculpture titled The Kiss was removed from an artwork inspired by this author
a figure originally based on this author appears seated below The Three Shades and placing his hand under his chin

clues.clue: Delacroix's Barque of Dante: Dante cowering from figures chewing on a boat; figure in brown cloak steadies him
quotes: That painting, which depicts this author cowering away from some figures chewing on a boat, is by Eugene Delacroix
A figure in a brown cloak steadies this author in a painting whose background depicts a burning city

clues.clue: Bouguereau's Prix de Rome entry: nude man biting another man's throat
quotes: William Bouguereau submitted a painting inspired by this author that depicts a nude man biting another man's throat

clues.clue: Domenico di Michelino painting in Florence Cathedral: Dante in red gown gestures to a chasm
quotes: That painting on the north wall of this building is by Domenico di Michelino

clues.clue: Liszt wrote Dante Sonata and Dante Symphony
quotes: this author whose Inferno inspired an eponymous piano sonata and choral symphony by Liszt

clues.clue: Mary Jo Bang translation alludes to Elton John ('a candle in the wind')
quotes: One translation of this author alludes to Elton John by referring to lives snuffed out like 'a candle in the wind' and is by Mary Jo Bang

works.name: La Vita Nuova
works.description: An early prosimetrum (mixed prose and poetry) written in the dolce stil novo ('sweet new style'). Describes Dante's love for Beatrice Portinari.

clues.clue: Describes first sight of Beatrice Portinari; Love feeds Dante's heart to Beatrice in a vision
quotes: Dante describes his first sight of Beatrice in a collection titled for a 'new' one of these things
In a vision, Love feeds this poet's heart to his beloved, Beatrice Portinari

clues.clue: Character Love confronts narrator clothed in fiery cloud, saying 'Ego dominus tuus' ('I am your master')
quotes: Love first confronts the narrator of La Vita Nuova while clothed in a fiery cloud... this character tells the speaker, 'Ego dominus tuus,' or 'I am your master'

clues.clue: Written in the dolce stil novo ('sweet new style'); a prosimetrum on courtly love
quotes: this poet's prosimetrum on courtly love written in the 'sweet new style'
This poet wrote of how 'Love hath so long possessed me for his own' in La Vita Nuova

clues.clue: Begins by noting the 'incipit' in 'that part of the book of my memory'
quotes: begins by noting the 'incipit' in 'that part of the book of my memory'

clues.clue: Dante Gabriel Rossetti made an 1861 English translation; painted Siddal as Beatrice
quotes: made an 1861 translation of a book by him
The poet of 'The Blessed Damozel' often painted his wife Elizabeth Siddal as this poet's muse

works.name: The Convivio (The Banquet)
works.description: An unfinished philosophical work featuring two sections praising Lady Philosophy, inspired by Boethius.

clues.clue: Unfinished work with two sections on Boethius-inspired praise for Lady Philosophy
quotes: This author wrote two sections themed on a Boethius-inspired praise for Lady Philosophy in an unfinished work titled 'The Banquet'

clues.clue: Refers to the traditional account that Eros is the son of Venus
quotes: This poet's Convivio refers to the more traditional account that Eros is the son of Venus

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Medieval Italian poet from Florence, part of the dolce stil novo movement. Friend of Guido Cavalcanti. His name was taken by Dante Gabriel Rossetti. T.S. Eliot was obsessed with him.

clues.clue: T.S. Eliot obsession: Prufrock opens with six-line Inferno quote; Waste Land calls Pound 'the better craftsman'; Little Gidding in terza rima; essays 'Dante' and 'What Dante Means to Me'
quotes: 'The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock' opens with a six line quote from a poem by this author
in 'The Waste Land', the speaker calls Ezra Pound 'the better craftsman', in reference to a poem by this man

clues.clue: Close friend of Guido Cavalcanti (dolce stil novo)
quotes: This friend of Cavalcanti wrote of how 'Love hath so long possessed me for his own'
the narrator meets Cavalcanti's father in the sixth circle of Hell

clues.clue: Samuel Beckett wrote 'Dante... Bruno. Vico.. Joyce' and 'Dante and the Lobster'
quotes: an essay whose title names these three Italians. That essay's title uses periods to indicate the centuries between these three Italians and Joyce
A character takes one of these animals to his Italian lesson... in a Samuel Beckett story titled 'Dante and' one of these animals

clues.clue: Dante Gabriel Rossetti took his name; Maria Francesca Rossetti wrote The Shadow of Dante
quotes: Christina Rossetti's brother took the name of what poet
The sibling of a more famous admirer of this poet composed a study of this poet's 'world' and 'pilgrimage,' titled for his 'Shadow'

clues.clue: One of the 'three crowns' of Italian literature (with Petrarch and Boccaccio)
quotes: Giosue Carducci wrote that this author looked outward, while the other two of the 'three crowns' looked inward and upward

clues.clue: Paul Valery argued about philosophy in Dante's poetry; T.S. Eliot tried to disprove it
quotes: That essay named for this man attempts to disprove an argument put forth by Paul Valery about philosophy in poetry

clues.clue: Graham Harman wrote about the 'broken hammer' of Dante (Object-Oriented Ontology)
quotes: Graham Harman has written about the 'broken hammer' of this Italian writer of the Divine Comedy

cross_refs.name: The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock
cross_refs.work: The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock

summary: Dante Alighieri is a medieval Italian poet best known for The Divine Comedy, an epic poem in three parts (Inferno, Purgatorio, Paradiso) written in terza rima, a verse form he invented consisting of stanzas with 11-syllable lines where the first and third lines rhyme and the second line rhymes with the first and last lines of the next stanza.
